items.text:
**Problem:** myco gets stuck whenever WebFetch is used on production (reported by @kkrazy 2026-05-18).

**Repro status:** Does NOT reproduce on this server. Subagent test fired `WebFetch("https://example.com")` → returned in ~3 s with ~30 byte body, clean SDK round-trip through `_canUseTool` → `_handlePermissionRequest` → `agent-event`. Historical events.jsonl across 3 prior session archives shows WebFetch listed in `system_init.tools` but ZERO actual `tool_use` events ever fired — so no past hangs in the local archives either.

**Static-analysis findings:** No WebFetch-specific code path. It uses the same `_canUseTool` flow as every other tool (Bash/Read/Edit/Grep). Permission menu emits via `_emit({type:'permission_request'...})` + `emit('menu',...)`, fanned to all attached WSes. SDK options passed: `permissionMode:'default'`, no `allowedTools`/`disallowedTools`, no WebFetch-specific settings.

**Likely root causes** (ranked by probability):
1. Permission menu doesn't render on reporter's client (CSS / device-specific) → callback promise never resolves → indefinite hang from the outside
2. Specific URL is slow/unreachable from prod server (no per-call timeout on `canUseTool`)
3. Different SDK version / settings on prod vs this server
4. Huge tool_result content jams the client browser (chrome batch with multi-MB body)

**Need from reporter to advance:**
- Which URL was being fetched
- Which server (myco.labxnow.ai / mycobeta.labxnow.ai / other)
- Was the permission menu visible in the chat pane at the time of the hang?
- Browser console errors
- If reproducible, attach output of `./collect-logs.sh --skip-mycobeta` grepped for `WebFetch|permission_request|agent-hook`

**Possible fixes once root cause confirmed:**
- `[diag-webfetch]` instrumentation (low-risk, ~30 lines, no behavior change) to capture next repro
- 30s timeout on `canUseTool` promises with auto-deny + log
- Cap `tool_result.content` at 64 KB in the wire frame (full content still in events.jsonl)

comments.text: please verify if this is still aproblem

**Closed as resolved via bug-21 fix chain.**

Verified against this session's `events.jsonl` (45 WebFetch-related events across 37 unique `tool_use` ids). Only one actual orphan permission_request exists across the entire history — the bug-21 incident at seq 3103/3104/3107/3108 (tool_use `sUN8qq3Dno` at 05:57:17). Every other WebFetch round-trip completed cleanly. The remaining "orphans" in the initial count are subagent-internal `tool_use` events that legitimately don't fire a parent-level `tool_result`.

The bug-21 fix chain (shipped, awaiting deploy) addresses the WebFetch hang root cause directly:

- **0eb1289** `bug-21`: parallel `canUseTool` no longer overwrites the menu slot (`pendingMenus` Map replaces single-slot `pendingMenu`). This was the actual symptom — two near-simultaneous permission_requests stomped each other, leaving one promise hanging indefinitely.
- **4ed02cd** `bug-14 round 2`: `abortController` typo fix — Stop now actually aborts a mid-flight WebFetch instead of letting it stream to completion.
- **72d7117** `bug-21 round 2`: `iteration_aborted` / `fatal` events terminate queue entries (so a stuck WebFetch can be cancelled out of the run-queue cleanly).
- **b243cc1** `fr-48`: dispatch marker prefix — `_activeRunItem` actually binds, so queue auto-advance fires on terminal events.

Proactive defenses listed in the original report (30 s `canUseTool` timeout, 64 KB `tool_result` cap, `[diag-webfetch]` instrumentation) are NOT shipped in this closure — they're documented as belt-and-braces for the unobserved root causes (#2, #3, #4 in the report). If WebFetch hangs recur post-deploy of the bug-21 fix chain, re-open with a fresh repro and we'll file those as a follow-up fr.

items.text:
**Problem:** myco's wire protocol (server `attach.js` ↔ client `app.js`) is ad-hoc — `JSON.stringify({ t: 'agent-event', event })` everywhere, flat event objects, no shared schema. Adding a new event type means editing the server emitter AND the client switch-statement AND remembering all the places that filter/route by `event.type`. Easy to drift.

**Source:** multica `server/pkg/protocol/{messages.go, events.go}`. Single `Message{Type string, Payload json.RawMessage}` envelope, ~13 event-category constants grouped by domain (`task:dispatch`, `task:progress`, `chat:message`, …). Decoupling envelope from typed payload lets you add events without touching the dispatcher.

**Expected:** A `server/src/protocol.js` (and matching `web/public/protocol.js`) module with:
- Event-type string constants (`AGENT_EVENT`, `CHAT`, `STATE_UPDATE`, `PRESENCE`, `MENU_PICK`, …)
- Typed payload shapes (JSDoc or TS-comment style) per event
- Helper builders: `protocol.agentEvent(ev)` returns `{ t: 'agent-event', event: ev }` etc.
- Helper decoders: `protocol.decode(json)` returns `{ kind, payload }`

**Why now (multi-user context):** With viewers + owner + multiple invited users attached to a session, the wire surface is wider than it was when myco was just owner-only. Each new frame type currently risks viewer parity bugs like `bug-13` (viewers missed agent-event for months). A typed catalog forces every new frame to be considered against every attach path.

**Effort:** ~½ day. Server side smaller (~50 lines refactor); client side bigger (~200-300 lines reducer cleanup). Tests: schema-shape tests + a regression that every WS frame the server emits matches a registered protocol type.

**Source code to study:** `multica-ai/multica` repo, `server/pkg/protocol/messages.go` and `server/pkg/protocol/events.go`.

**Problem:** myco's `attach.js` fans every `agent-event` / `chat` / `state-update` frame to every WS attached to a session, regardless of relevance. With bug-13 widening viewer fan-out, a session with 5 attached clients (owner + 4 viewers) ships every event 5×. There's no per-client subscription filter — viewers who only care about chat still receive every tool_use blob.

**Source:** multica `server/internal/daemonws/hub.go` + `server/internal/realtime/broadcaster.go`. They split daemon-facing (agent processes reporting in) from browser-facing (UIs observing) WebSockets, with scope types `workspace / user / task / chat / daemon_runtime`. Clients subscribe per-scope; broadcaster fans only to matching subs.

**Expected for myco** (revised: myco is multi-user, this is directly applicable now, not future):
- Subscription frame on attach: `{ t:'sub', scopes:['chat','agent-event','presence'] }` — viewer can omit `agent-event` if they only care about chat
- Server tracks per-WS scope set; `_broadcastTo(sessionId, scope, frame)` instead of `session.on('agent-event', onAgentEvent)` blanket subscribe
- Default scope set per role: owner=all, viewer=all (preserves bug-13 behavior), share-link=chat+presence only (privacy floor)
- Presence chip surfaces per-viewer scope set so owners can see who is listening to what

**Why it matters for multi-user myco:** Wire-volume scales with attached-client count today; scoped subscriptions make a 10-viewer session cheap instead of 10× duplicated. Also unblocks future "silent observer" modes (analytics, replay tooling) that need a subset of events.

**Out of scope:** redis/pubsub multi-node fan-out (multica has this; myco is single-host, won't need it until horizontal scale).

**Effort:** ~1 day. Net diff is moderate — touches every `session.on(...)` in attach.js plus the client's WS connect path. Tests: assert viewer with chat-only scope does NOT receive agent-event frames.

**Problem:** Today, WS event handlers in `web/public/app.js` patch in-memory state directly (`state.artifacts.byType[type] = artifact`, `_appendAgentEvent(ev)` mutates DOM). When a frame is dropped or arrives out of order across N attached clients, each client's local state drifts from server truth — exactly the class of bug that `bug-9`, `bug-11`, `ryan-blues stale-plan` were instances of.

**Source:** multica CLAUDE.md hard rule — "WebSocket events invalidate TanStack Query keys rather than writing directly to Zustand stores." Combined with workspace-scoped query-key convention. WS frames carry a `(scope, id)` tuple; the client's reaction is `queryClient.invalidateQueries([scope, id])` → React re-fetches; no in-memory patching.

**Expected for myco:**
- A small `state-cache.js` layer (no need for TanStack Query — we don't use React) that wraps the existing HTTP fetches (`/sessions/:id/artifact`, `/sessions/:id/chat/history`, `/sessions`) with cache + staleness
- WS frames trigger `cache.invalidate(key)` instead of patching `state.*` directly
- Reactive layer re-fetches + re-renders the affected pane

**Why it matters for multi-user myco:** Every attached client today does its own in-memory bookkeeping from the WS stream. When user A's tab patches `state.chatMessages` from a chat frame, user B's tab does the same — independently. Drift across clients is invisible unless someone refreshes. With invalidation-driven re-reads, server is the single source of truth, every client converges to the same view on next render.

**Trade-off:** more HTTP round-trips. Mitigated by ETag / If-Modified-Since on the artifact / chat-history endpoints (server can short-circuit unchanged responses with 304).

**Effort:** ~2 days. Touches every WS frame handler in app.js. Tests: drop a WS frame and verify the next render still matches server state.

**Problem:** myco's session/task dispatch paths (session spawn, `/run` plan-item dispatch, chat→agent forwarding) don't have an explicit "acquire capacity slot BEFORE claiming work" invariant documented or enforced. If any of these paths claims work before confirming it has the slot/budget to run it, the work piles up in a `dispatched` state with no `StartTask` follow-up — silent loss.

**Source:** multica `server/internal/daemon/daemon.go runRuntimePoller` + `handleTask`. Their code comment explicitly warns: *"acquiring the execution slot AFTER ClaimTask causes tasks to pile up in dispatched state with no StartTask."* They use a per-runtime semaphore acquired first, claim second.

**Expected for myco:**
- Audit the 3 candidate paths: `sessions.spawnSession`, `slashcmds.handleRunPlanItem`, `attach.handleChatMessage` (claude-forward branch)
- For each, identify what acts as the "capacity slot" (max-concurrent-sessions, SDK iteration in-progress, etc.)
- Reorder so slot is acquired BEFORE claim, never after
- Add a stable comment marker (e.g. `// SLOT-THEN-CLAIM (multica-pattern)`) at each enforcement site
- Static-grep test in test.sh asserting the marker exists at all known dispatch sites — catches future code that reverses the order

**Why it matters for multi-user myco:** Multiple users sharing a session = more concurrent dispatch events than the single-user case ever produced. The hazard is more likely to fire under multi-user load. Also: shared sessions mean dropped work is a worse failure (multiple users notice the loss).

**Effort:** ~½ day for the audit + comment markers + grep test. Larger if any path actually has the inverted order and needs refactoring.

**Reference:** Read multica's `runRuntimePoller` (~30 lines) for the canonical pattern.

**Problem:** Codex / Gemini / Cursor / other coding agents look for `AGENTS.md` at repo root; Claude Code looks for `CLAUDE.md`. Maintaining two files = drift risk. myco's project-spawn flow + best-practices-template.md only handle `CLAUDE.md` today.

**Source:** multica does this. Their `AGENTS.md` is a one-liner: `# See CLAUDE.md`. Other-agent users get the same instructions for free, with zero duplication or sync risk.

**Expected:**
- Add `AGENTS.md` at the myco repo root with `# See [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) for project instructions.`
- Update `injectBestPracticesIntoClaudeMd` in `server/src/sessions.js` to ALSO create/maintain `AGENTS.md` redirect file in spawned project dirs (idempotent: if AGENTS.md exists with non-redirect content, leave it alone)
- Add a static-grep test: `test -f AGENTS.md` exists at repo root + content matches the redirect pattern

**Why it matters for myco specifically (multi-agent context):** myco's sessions are agent-native, and the auto-injected CLAUDE.md gives consistent guidance. Right now that consistency is Claude-only. As users mix agents — running Codex in one session, Claude in another — AGENTS.md ensures the non-Claude tools see the same project-shaped rules without us maintaining two templates.

**Effort:** ~10 min for the repo-root file + commit. Optional second pass (~½ hour) for the per-project mirror in sessions.js + test.

**Out of scope:** Maintaining a fully-separate AGENTS.md template with agent-tool-neutral phrasing. The CLAUDE.md content is already mostly agent-tool-neutral (just says "the agent" not "Claude" in most rules); a redirect is sufficient until that breaks down.

**Problem:** myco dual-writes session state — `/data/sessions.json` (registry, chat, allow/deny) AND `<project>/_myco_/events.jsonl` (agent-event log). They desync on crashes; plan-item updates, chat appends, run-outcomes each have their own persistence path with no single durable order.

**Source:** [OpenHands](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands). Every state change = typed `Event` (Action vs Observation) appended to `EventLog`. In-memory state = FOLD over the log; restart = re-fold from snapshot + tail.

**Expected for myco:**
- New `server/src/event-store.js`: `append(type, payload)` + `fold(handlers)` + `snapshot(every N)`
- ALL state mutations route through it: chat, plan-item edit, allow-list update, run-outcome
- `sessions.json` becomes a derived projection rebuilt on startup (or from periodic snapshot)
- Crash recovery = replay log from last snapshot deterministically

**Why for multi-user myco:** Two clients editing simultaneously (owner adds td-N while viewer comments on bug-M) can race the sessions.json write today. Append-only log serializes naturally; fold layer resolves conflicts.

**Files touched:** `agent-session.js` (1042 lines, emit via store), `sessions.js` (~700, loadStore/saveStore L66-79 becomes projection), `artifacts.js` (plan/test/arch route through store). One-shot migration script reads existing dual-write state, rebuilds unified log.

**Effort:** L (~2 days). Tests: kill server mid-write, restart, assert state matches last appended event.

**Problem:** Chat messages in myco are atomic envelopes — a tool result streaming over 10s re-renders the whole bubble each time. Reconnecting viewers see partial state as fully-replaced rows, losing scroll position. Tool input + tool result + assistant text flatten into one agent-event blob with no per-part id for partial updates.

**Source:** [OpenCode (sst)](https://github.com/sst/opencode). Their `messages` + `parts` model: messages are envelopes; "parts" (text-part, tool-call-part, tool-result-part, status-part) are independently streamable atoms, each with stable id. Server broadcasts part-by-part; client patches in place by id.

**Expected for myco:**
- Typed envelope carrying `parts: [{ id, type, ... }]`
- Part types: `assistant-text`, `tool-call`, `tool-result`, `status`, `error`
- Server emits `{ t:'part-add'|'part-update'|'part-done', sessionId, msgId, part }` instead of monolithic agent-event
- Client renderer keys by `part.id` — incremental updates patch the right DOM node
- Viewer reconnect: server replays missing parts via `?afterPartSeq=N` (extends existing `?afterSeq`)

**Why for multi-user myco:** Multiple viewers watching a slow tool call see whole-bubble re-renders out of order today. Part-keyed patches converge to identical DOM across all attached clients without race.

**Depends on:** `fr-27` (typed wire protocol). Ideally land them together so new part frames are catalogued from day one.

**Files touched:** `agent-session.js` (split tool_result into per-part fragments), `attach.js` (new `t:'part-*'` frame family), `app.js` (~8077 lines, chat-bubble renderer becomes part-keyed).

**Effort:** L (~3 days). Tests: part arrives out of order → final DOM still correct.

**Problem:** When a user wants to "try a different approach" mid-session (e.g. revert before the last 3 turns + explore a different prompt), the only path is to start a new session — losing context, chat history, plan items, workspace edits. No native fork / checkpoint mechanism.

**Source:** [opcode (winfunc)](https://github.com/winfunc/opcode). Tauri 2 / React desktop GUI for Claude Code with branching checkpoint timeline (`src-tauri/src/checkpoint/`). User snapshots at turn N, forks, switches branches, diffs any two checkpoints.

**Expected for myco:**
- New `server/src/checkpoints.js`: `snapshot(sessionId, turnId, label?)`, `fork(sessionId, ckId)`, `list(sessionId)`, `restore(sessionId, ckId)`, `diff(sessionId, ckA, ckB)`
- Storage: `<project>/_myco_/checkpoints/<turn>/` containing truncated `events.jsonl` snapshot + git-stash of workspace tree (use git plumbing, not new VCS)
- Forked session = sibling session id sharing checkpoint pointer; events diverge into the new session's events.jsonl
- UI: timeline gutter in chat pane with Fork / Restore / Diff actions

**Why for multi-user myco:** When multiple users collaborate on a session and someone wants to explore an alternative without disrupting others, fork lets them branch off without losing shared history. Useful for /run exploration of multiple plan-item approaches.

**Files touched:** new `checkpoints.js` (~200 lines); `sessions.js` (+ `parentSessionId` + `forkedAt` fields); `agent-session.js` (honor `forkedFromCheckpoint` on spawn); `app.js` (timeline gutter UI). New routes: `POST /sessions/:id/checkpoint`, `POST /sessions/:id/fork/:ckId`, `GET /sessions/:id/checkpoints`, `GET /sessions/:id/diff?a=&b=`.

**Effort:** L+ (~4 days; biggest piece is git-worktree integration for the workspace side). Tests: snapshot → mutate → restore → assert state matches snapshot.

**Problem:** myco's `permissions.js`, `myco-mcp.js`, and other modules hold process-global state (registries, in-flight tool calls, allow-list caches) implicitly shared across all concurrent sessions. As multi-user concurrency grows, shared-mutable state is a footgun: a permission update in session A can race a permission check in session B; the MCP server's plan-item appender uses a session-id closure but the surrounding module is singleton.

**Source:** [Goose (block)](https://github.com/block/goose). They migrated from a global `AgentRef` to a per-`session_id` `AgentManager` with own ExtensionManager / ToolMonitor / channels. [Discussion #4389](https://github.com/block/goose/discussions/4389) documents the motivation: eliminate global lock contention when many sessions run concurrently.

**Expected for myco:**
- Introduce `class SessionContext` in `agent-session.js` carrying: `sessionId`, `permissionsCache`, `mcpServer`, `toolMonitor`, per-session event bus
- Refactor `permissions.js` (~180 lines) to export `createPermissionsForSession(sessionId)` factories instead of singleton methods
- Refactor `myco-mcp.js` (~152 lines) so per-session closure becomes a SessionContext member, not closure-captured variable
- Audit other modules for hidden globals: `summarizer.js`, `transcript.js`, `slashcmds.js`

**Why for multi-user myco:** Today a permission allow-list mutation in session A's chat could be observed mid-check by session B if they share in-memory cache. Per-session isolation prevents whole class of cross-session leakage. Cheap to do BEFORE concurrency grows; painful after (every change backported into per-session shape).

**Effort:** M (~1.5 days). Tests: spawn 5 concurrent SessionContexts, mutate permissions in each, assert no cross-contamination.

**Problem:** Users repeatedly run the same multi-step workflows by hand — "kick off N-thread architecture review," "extract patterns from project X via WebFetch + summarise into _myco_/research/," "file a bug + write regression test first per BP §5 + commit." These workflows live in tribal knowledge or ad-hoc chat prompts; they're not shareable, not parameterised, not versioned.

**Source:** [Goose (block)](https://github.com/block/goose). "Recipes" = YAML files containing: prompt template + required extensions + parameters + retry policy. Subrecipes nest as isolated sub-sessions whose summaries return to parent orchestrator. Portable, version-controlled, composable.

**Expected for myco:**
- New `_myco_/recipes/*.yaml` per-project recipe dir (auto-injected like CLAUDE.md)
- Recipe shape:
  ```yaml
  name: arch-review
  params: [target_branch]
  steps:
    - subagent: general-purpose
      prompt: "Review {target_branch} for X"
      verify: "output contains 'Recommendations:'"
  ```
- Slash command `/recipe <name> [params]` in `slashcmds.js` (~1042 lines) to invoke
- Subrecipes nest as isolated sub-sessions (via Agent tool); summaries return to parent's chat
- New Plan-tab subview: "Recipes" — list / run / view past runs
- Plan-item integration: a plan item can declare a recipe to run on `/run`

**Why for multi-user myco:** Recipes are inherently team artifacts — one user authors, all users (and agent on /run dispatch) can invoke. Multi-user becomes the WHY: single-user setup has less need to crystallise workflows; team setup amplifies leverage.

**Files touched:** new `recipes.js` (~200 lines); `slashcmds.js` (/recipe handler); `sessions.js spawnSession` (seed `_myco_/recipes/` on spawn); new UI subview in `app.js`.

**Effort:** M+ (~2 days). Tests: invoke recipe with params, verify subagent fires, verify summary returns to parent chat.

**Problem:** When `web/public/best-practices-template.md` is updated (e.g. §7+§8 in `ccb5625`+`103bc88`), the new content reaches ONLY new projects spawned after the deploy + the Arch-tab UI banner on browser refresh. Existing projects whose CLAUDE.md already contains `<!-- myco-best-practices-start -->` KEEP the old template forever — the agent reads stale content on every turn across full deploys.

**Root cause:** `injectBestPracticesIntoClaudeMd` in `server/src/sessions.js:514` short-circuits `if (existing.includes(BP_SENTINEL_START)) return;` to protect user hand-edits. Side effect: zero update propagation.

**Expected:**
- Stamp each injection with `<!-- myco-bp-hash:<sha256-of-template> -->` inside the block
- On call, if block exists: compare embedded hash vs current template hash
- Match → no-op
- Differ → re-inject new template, preserving text bracketed by NEW inner markers `<!-- myco-bp-custom-start -->` ... `<!-- myco-bp-custom-end -->` where teammates put hand-edits
- Fresh injections embed BOTH markers so users have a documented place for additions

**Files:** `server/src/sessions.js` (~30 lines added); `web/public/best-practices-template.md` (header note about custom-marker pair); `test/best-practices-inject.test.js` (drift triggers re-inject, custom block survives, matching-hash no-ops).

**Migration:** existing CLAUDE.md files lack new markers — first post-deploy attach treats missing hash as drift, re-injects with markers + current content. Idempotent thereafter.

**Effort:** ~½ day. Strict superset of current behavior.

**Payoff:** §7+§8 shipped this session won't reach existing projects without this. Future template edits propagate automatically on next attach.

**Problem:** Ad-hoc messages to Claude can trigger code/functionality changes without an approved work item gating them.
**Expected:** All code changes must be triggered by an approved td/fr/bug.
**Actual:** Arbitrary chat messages to Claude can initiate code or functionality changes with no approved td/fr/bug backing them.

meta.originalText: all code changes should be triggered by an approved td/fr/bug. No adhoc msg to claude should trigger any code/functionality change.

**Problem:** Claude Code SDK usage in myco may lag behind the multica implementation, but the gaps aren't catalogued.

comments.text:
🔍 **Analysis** — myco vs multica Claude Code SDK integration (no code shipped — report only per request)

## 1. Top-line caveat — apples-to-oranges

These two systems use **fundamentally different integration strategies** with Claude Code:

| | myco | multica |
|---|---|---|
| Library | `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (Node, in-process) | None — execs the `claude` CLI as a subprocess |
| Process model | One Node process owns N `query()` async-iterators | Daemon spawns one `claude --output-format stream-json` subprocess per task |
| Agent support | Claude only | Claude, Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, Hermes, Kimi, Kiro, OpenClaw, OpenCode, Pi |
| Language | JS (~900 lines `agent-session.js`) | Go (`server/pkg/agent/*.go` — one file per CLI behind a `Backend` interface) |
| Permission style | In-process callback (`canUseTool` + `PreToolUse` hook), interactive chat-pane menus | `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` (autonomous), no interactive prompts |
| MCP | SDK `createSdkMcpServer` (in-process) | `--mcp-config <path>` to external JSON config |
| Session resume | `sdkOpts.resume = sdkSessionId` | `--resume <sessionID>` CLI flag |

**Implication:** many multica patterns (multi-agent backend abstraction, CLI subprocess management, stderr tail buffers, idle watchdog) don't transfer because myco lives inside the SDK's process. Conversely, myco's structured-event richness + interactive permission UX would be hard for multica to match because they only see the CLI's stream-json output.

The genuinely portable patterns are: process death detection, the per-task usage counter, the multi-backend `Provider` taxonomy (if myco ever adds Codex/Cursor), and the bounded stderr-tail buffer.

## 2. Capability-by-capability comparison

| Capability | myco today | multica | Gap / opportunity | Pri+Eff |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Session lifecycle / resume** | `sdkSessionId` persisted on `rec.sdkSessionId` (sessions.js:312); first `system/init` event captures it (agent-session.js:300-318); `_ensureIteration` passes `sdkOpts.resume = sdkSessionId` (agent-session.js:247). Hydrates event buffer from `_myco_/events.jsonl` on respawn. | Daemon stores `PriorSessionID` on Task; passes `--resume <id>`; on resume failure, `resolveSessionID()` clears the id and the daemon **retries with a fresh session**. | myco assumes resume always works; if the SDK errors on a stale `sdkSessionId` it surfaces as `fatal`. **Add fallback: catch resume-failure error, clear `sdkSessionId`, retry once with fresh session.** | P1 / S |
| **Tool permissions** | Two-stage: `PreToolUse` hook checks per-session allow/deny list; falls through to `canUseTool` which renders a chat-pane menu. MCP `mcp__myco__*` auto-allowed. | `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` — autonomous, no prompts. Squad-level approval gating happens server-side before the CLI is even spawned. | myco's interactive ladder is **richer than multica's**. Real gap: no trust-ladder beyond per-pattern allow/deny. fr-38's strict-mode is the analogue of multica's server-side gate. | P2 / S |
| **Custom tools / MCP** | One in-process SDK MCP server per session (`createSdkMcpServer`, myco-mcp.js:118). Only one tool: `mcp__myco__add_plan_items`. | External MCP via `--mcp-config <jsonPath>`. Out-of-process. | myco's approach is **dramatically lower-latency** + easier to author (Zod schema). Missing: more tools — `mcp__myco__update_plan_item_status`, `mcp__myco__add_comment`, `mcp__myco__search_plan`, `mcp__myco__post_run_summary`. Each replaces an "ask claude to call Edit on plan.json" workaround. | P1 / M |

Actual gap: **fr-33 (parts model)** — tool results still monolithic. Also: `unknown_event` is passthrough — new SDK event types silently fall into the catch-all. | P2 / S |

Concurrency hazard documented in fr-35. | Each task = separate `agent.New()` Backend, separate subprocess. **Hard isolation by OS process boundary.** Daemon limits via `MULTICA_TASK_SLOT`. | myco's shared-process model **wins on latency + memory** but loses on isolation. Genuine gap: **no concurrency cap.** A user spamming `/run` could spawn N parallel SDK iterations and bury the rate-limit. Add a max-concurrent-iterations gate. fr-35's SessionContext refactor is the structural fix. | P1 / M |
| **Memory / project context** | Per-session `.claude/memory/` via `settings.autoMemoryDirectory`; `settingSources: ['project','local']` excludes shared user tier. `injectBestPracticesIntoClaudeMd` seeds template on spawn + respawn. | No CLAUDE.md detail in README; relies on `--append-system-prompt` + skills system. | myco's per-session memory **wins clearly.** Gap: memory propagation when the template changes (fr-37 filed). Also: a way to share memory across sibling sessions of same user. | P2 / M |
| **Subagents / delegation** | Implicitly available — the SDK's Task tool works inside the iteration; no special myco wiring. fr-36 (recipes) would be the structured layer above it. | "Squads" — leader agent routes work to other agents. Architectural multi-agent coordination at the server-task level. | myco has **no first-class subagent surface** — no "spawn a subagent + bring the summary back to chat." Subagent summaries appear in transcript jsonl but are not surfaced in the chat pane. Add an `assistant_text` variant for subagent results. | P2 / S |
| **Error recovery** | Single try/catch around `for await (m of stream)` (agent-session.js:260). AbortError → `iteration_aborted`; everything else → `fatal`. **No retry on transient errors.** | Daemon has idle-watchdog, bounded stderr tail, retry-on-resume-failure. `withAgentStderr` enriches errors with last-N-lines of stderr for diagnosis. | Two real gaps: (a) **no auto-retry on `rate_limit_event`** (currently just emitted); (b) **no stderr tail equivalent** — when the SDK throws, the error message is `String(err.message)` only. Wrap iteration in retry-with-backoff for rate-limit + network. Capture last-N events into the fatal payload. | P1 / S |
| **Observability** | Console logs prefixed `[agent-hook]`, `[agent-menu]`, etc. `logCapture.js` rolling 5000-line buffer exposed via `GET /logs`. | Daemon has `metrics` package (separate from `events`/`logger`); `analytics` package for usage events. `health.go` exposes `/health` with uptime + active task count. | myco's marker-prefix discipline is good but ad-hoc. Gap: **no per-session metrics endpoint.** Add `GET /sessions/:id/metrics` returning `{turns, toolCalls, errors, lastRateLimit, avgTurnMs}`. Source data already in `turn_result` events. | P2 / M |
| **Cost tracking** | `turn_result` event carries `usage`, `totalCostUsd`, `durationMs`, `numTurns` (agent-session.js:412). Surfaced as an event but **not aggregated into session-level totals** anywhere queryable. | `TaskUsageEntry` type (per-model token usage); `map[string]TokenUsage` handler signatures — per-task accumulator. | Gap: **myco never accumulates `totalCostUsd` per session.** A user can't see "this session has cost \$4.20 across 47 turns." Trivial to add: maintain `rec.usage = {totalUsd, totalTokens, byModel}`, increment on each `turn_result`, surface in attach snapshot. | P1 / S |

## 3. Prioritized work items (potential `fr-*` candidates)

1. **fr-N: Resume-failure fallback for stale `sdkSessionId`.** Catch the SDK error class on resume, clear `rec.sdkSessionId`, retry once with fresh session, emit a `resume_failed` event. **Files:** `agent-session.js` `_ensureIteration` try/catch (lines 250-272). **P1 / S (~½ day).**
2. **fr-N: Per-session cost + usage rollup.** Maintain `rec.usage = {totalUsd, totalTokens:{input,output,cache}, byModel:{}, turnCount}`. Increment on every `turn_result`. Add to `getSessionRecord` payload + render in chat-pane header. **P1 / S (~1 day).**
3. **fr-N: Expand the in-process MCP toolbox.** Add `mcp__myco__update_plan_item_status`, `mcp__myco__add_comment`, `mcp__myco__post_run_summary`, `mcp__myco__search_plan`. Each replaces an "ask claude to call Edit on plan.json" anti-pattern. **P1 / M (~1.5 days).**
4. **fr-N: Rate-limit + transient-error retry.** Wrap `for await (m of stream)` with retry-on-rate-limit (read `m.retry_after`); back off, re-spawn `query()` with `resume=`. Cap at 3 retries. **P1 / S (~½ day).**
5. **fr-N: Per-session concurrency cap.** Gate `_ensureIteration` behind a global semaphore (configurable, default 8). When at cap, queue or emit `iteration_queued` event. **P1 / M (~1 day).**
6. **fr-N: Stderr-equivalent diagnostic tail in `fatal` events.** Capture the last 20 emitted events into `fatal.recentEvents` (parallel to multica's `agentStderrTailBytes`). **P2 / S (~½ day).**
7. **fr-N: Subagent result surfacing.** When the SDK emits an `assistant_text` from a Task-tool subagent, tag it with `meta.subagent:true` so the chat pane can render it distinctly. **P2 / S (~1 day).**

## 4. Things multica does that **wouldn't** make sense for myco

- **Multi-CLI backend abstraction (`Backend` interface across 11 agents).** myco is intentionally Claude-only; the SDK gives much better fidelity than CLI scraping.
- **`--permission-mode bypassPermissions`.** myco's interactive trust-ladder is a *feature*, not a bug.
- **External `--mcp-config` JSON.** In-process MCP via `createSdkMcpServer` is strictly better — lower latency, type-safe via Zod, no subprocess management.
- **Daemon-on-your-machine + server-in-cloud split.** myco runs both in one container; multica's split is necessary because their daemon shells out to local CLIs.
- **Squads / leader agents.** myco's per-session admin model + `/run` mechanism already covers the surface differently.
- **PostgreSQL + pgvector + sqlc.** myco's flat-file `sessions.json` + `events.jsonl` is appropriate for its scale; a DB migration is tracked in fr-32 (event-store refactor).

## 5. What myco does **better** than multica

- **Lower latency tool round-trips.** In-process MCP + in-process `canUseTool` callback skip the JSON-over-stdout-then-parse round-trip multica pays per tool call.
- **Richer permission UX.** Multica's `bypassPermissions` is all-or-nothing; myco's `Allow once / Allow always / Deny` trio + per-session deny list + chat-pane menu cards is genuinely better collaborative UX.
- **First-class chat persistence contract.** myco's `meta.seq` monotonic ordering + 3-tier byte-budget windowing + `afterSeq` catch-up is more sophisticated than anything visible in multica's events package.
- **Reattach-with-replay.** myco's `_shipAgentReplay` + `events.jsonl` hydrate lets a fresh browser tab on a different device see exactly what every other attached client sees, byte-for-byte. multica's docs say nothing about cross-device session attach.
- **In-process MCP authoring ergonomics.** myco-mcp.js:117-150 is ~30 lines to add a new tool; multica's external MCP requires authoring + deploying + path-configuring a separate process.
- **Per-session memory + settings sandbox.** `settings.autoMemoryDirectory` + `settingSources: ['project','local']` keeps user-tier `$HOME/.claude/settings.json` from leaking across sessions.

---

**Key files cited:** `server/src/agent-session.js` (SDK wiring), `server/src/myco-mcp.js` (in-process MCP server), `server/src/permissions.js` (allow/deny matcher), `server/src/menu.js` (menu → chat broadcast), `server/src/sessions.js` (spawn + resume + memory migration), `server/src/attach.js` (WS attach + replay shippers), `server/package.json` (`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@^0.3.142`).

**Multica references:** `github.com/multica-ai/multica` — `server/pkg/agent/{agent,claude,codex,gemini,cursor}.go`, `server/internal/daemon/{daemon,wakeup,health,types}.go`, README.

**Next step (not done in this analysis pass):** file the 7 prioritized items as discrete `fr-N` plan entries so they show up in the Plan tab for prioritization + voting.

items.text:
**Problem:** Permission menu lost when two `permission_request` events arrive within ~2s. myco-beta (sid `myco-kkrazy-f80476dd`) was stuck for ~50min on a hanging WebFetch `canUseTool` callback after the SDK issued two parallel WebFetch calls (`toolu_017j...` at 05:57:17 and `toolu_01So...` at 05:57:19).

**Expected:** Both pending permission requests render to the user; resolving one does not silently discard the other; `rec.permissionMenu` queues multiple concurrent menus keyed by `toolUseID`.

**Actual:** User resolved the SECOND via `allow-always`; the FIRST stayed pending forever. State desync: SDK process alive blocked on `canUseTool`, while `rec.pendingMenu` and `rec.permissionMenu` both `null` in `/data/sessions.json`. Three queued user prompts (06:27, 06:43, 06:45) never got processed.

**Root cause (suspected):** `rec.permissionMenu` is a single slot — the second arriving `permission_request` overwrites the first instead of queueing.

**Fix:** Change `rec.permissionMenu` to a `Map<toolUseID, menu>`, broadcast all pending menus to clients, resolve each independently. Also re-evaluate any pending `permission_request`s against newly-saved `allow-always` rules so a broad-scope rule auto-resolves earlier matching requests.

**Context:** Evidence in `/wks/kkrazy/myco-kkrazy-f80476dd/_myco_/events.jsonl` seq 3107–3110. Found while debugging stuck-session report against mycobeta.

**Related (urgent meta-bug):** `mcp__myco__add_plan_items` MCP tool writes back from a stale in-memory snapshot of `_myco_/plan.json`, clobbering any disk changes made between mycod boot and the tool call. Reproduced just now: an `add_plan_items` call after a `git pull` reverted bug-7's rewrite, td-21's rewrite, td-28's recent comment, and deleted fr-41 entirely. Workaround: edit `plan.json` directly when in-flight pulls or external edits have happened.

comments.text:
✅ **Fixed** in commit `0eb1289` — `bug-21: parallel canUseTool fires no longer deadlock the SDK`.

## Root causes (3 orthogonal, all required for the deadlock)

1. **`agent-session.js this.pendingMenu` was a single slot.** The 2nd `canUseTool` fire's menu overwrote the 1st.

2. **`menu.js broadcastMenuToChat` called `_supersedeStaleMenus` on every new menu broadcast.** Its comment assumed serial tool-call mode ("the SDK only fires a fresh canUseTool when the prior one has already been resolved") — false under parallel tool calls. The sweep marked the 1st menu's chat row as superseded the moment the 2nd menu landed, even though the 1st's resolver promise was still live.

3. **`attach.js` bare-digit chat shortcut used `session.pendingMenu.hash`** — only the most-recently-added menu was reachable via the digit shortcut. Per-card button-clicks (with their own hash) remained unambiguous, but the digit fallback could no longer reach the older pending menu.

Net symptom: the 1st canUseTool resolver promise was never settled, the SDK iteration blocked forever waiting on it, and queued user messages piled up unprocessable.

## Fix shape

- `agent-session.js` — `this.pendingMenus = new Map()` (Map<hash, menu>) replaces the single slot. Set/delete by hash so a settle on hash A leaves hash B alone. Back-compat `pendingMenu` getter returns the most-recently-added (matches the pre-fix read shape for casual readers like `slashcmds.handleDecide`). New `oldestPendingMenu` getter exposes the FIFO head-of-queue for the bare-digit shortcut.
- `agent-session.js` — new `_reevaluatePendingAfterAllowAlways()` method called from `resolveMenuPick` when `n===2` saves a fresh rule. Walks every still-pending permission entry; for each whose `(toolName, matchingInput)` now resolves to `'allow'` via `permissions.decide`, auto-settles with `{behavior:'allow'}` and emits `permission_resolved {auto:true, reason:'matched rule saved via sibling Allow always'}` so the chat-pane sees why the card auto-cleared. This is the bug-21 spec's "newly-saved allow-always rules retroactively resolve earlier matching requests."
- `menu.js` — removed the `_supersedeStaleMenus` call from `broadcastMenuToChat`. The function itself stays (still legitimately called from `sessions.ensureLiveSession` on AgentSession respawn — those menus genuinely refer to dead resolver promises from a killed process). What's removed is just the supersede-on-every-broadcast trigger.
- `attach.js` — bare-digit shortcut now reads `session.oldestPendingMenu` (FIFO). When `>1` menus pending, emits a clarifying chat note ("picked option N for the OLDEST of M pending menus — click a card's button to target it directly").

## Test coverage (per CLAUDE.md BP §6)

- **`test/bug-21-parallel-permission-menus.test.js`** (NEW, 16 cases) covers: independent Map storage, no single-slot overwrite, back-compat `pendingMenu` returns newest, bare-digit picks oldest (FIFO), no-pending reports cleanly, Allow always retroactively resolves matching sibling, no false-positive across tools, Deny doesn't trigger reevaluate, supersede-on-broadcast removed, 3-menu arbitrary-order resolution. Plus 6 static-grep guards anchoring the fix shape onto the prod source.
- **`test/state-update.test.js`** (UPDATED): the prior case "broadcastMenuToChat invokes `_supersedeStaleMenus` before the new row lands" pinned the OLD broken contract. Reversed to lock the bug-21 contract: `broadcastMenuToChat` must NOT call `_supersedeStaleMenus`. Same coverage shape, opposite assertion.

Regression sweep: 162 / 162 tests green across bug-21 + 11 adjacent suites (state-update, chat-routing, agent-replay-dedup, persist-assistant-chat, bug-17, chat-persistence-contract, admin-delegation, strict-mode-gate, stop-button-interrupt, plan-cache-session-isolation, viewer-agent-events).

## Caveats / followups

- The full `./test.sh` couldn't run end-to-end on the current container (busybox grep lacks `-Pzoq` for the static-checks phase). This is environmental, predates bug-21, and blocks any agent run on this host. Worth a separate fr to either install GNU grep on the build image or port the static checks to busybox-portable `node -e` regex.
- Bug-21's secondary mention of an "MCP stale-snapshot meta-bug" was filed AFTER my fix started and isn't addressed by this commit. If the snapshot mismatch is still observable post-deploy, file as a follow-up bug — different code path (MCP server snapshot vs. canUseTool promise resolution).

🔍 **Reopened** — investigation surfaced a SECOND, distinct root cause for the "research tasks hang" symptom.

## Evidence from this session's events.jsonl (3438 events, 65 respawns)

Two orphaned-tool-use patterns observed:

### Pattern 1 (already fixed in commit `0eb1289`)

| seq | time | event |
|---|---|---|
| 3088 | 05:57:02 | Agent dispatched — *"SDK best-practices research for myco"* |
| 3103 | 05:57:17 | Subagent fires WebFetch #1 (hash `017j…`) |
| 3104 | 05:57:17 | permission_request #1 |
| 3107 | 05:57:19 | Subagent fires WebFetch #2 (hash `01So…`) **2 sec later, parallel** |
| 3108 | 05:57:19 | permission_request #2 — overwrites pendingMenu single-slot |
| 3110 | 05:57:21 | User picks "Allow always" on what's visible (#2); #1 is orphaned |
| 3111 | 05:57:21 | tool_result for #2 only |
| 3128 | 07:01:23 | **5-min reaper kills the AgentSession after the user disconnected** — recovery, not resolution |

→ Fixed by `0eb1289` (Map<hash, menu>, FIFO bare-digit, remove supersede-on-broadcast, retroactive auto-resolve on Allow always).

### Pattern 2 (NEW — not addressed by 0eb1289)

| time | event |
|---|---|
| 13:19:34 | Agent dispatched — *"Re-refresh architecture.md for post-SDK migration"* |
| 13:19:34 → 13:26:37 | ~100 sub-tool-calls, all `tool_use`/`tool_result` pairs complete cleanly |
| **13:26:37 → 13:32:20** | **Subagent silent for ~5 min** (model thinking / synthesis / sub-API call — no events on parent stream) |
| 13:32:20 | **session_ready respawn** — 5-min reaper killed the AgentSession **while the subagent was still in flight**. Agent tool_use orphaned, no tool_result ever delivered to parent SDK iteration. |

The reaper logic (`attach.js _scheduleSessionKill`) measures **client disconnect**, not tool-activity. When the user's browser tab disconnects (closed tab, network blip, mobile background), the 5-min kill timer starts. On fire, `killSession` runs unconditionally — even if a tool_use is mid-execution. Long-running subagents (the research-task pattern) are the most exposed: they routinely have multi-minute internal phases where the parent stream is quiet.

## Frequency

In this session alone:
- **1** orphaned `permission_request` (bug-21 pattern 1)
- **2** orphaned `Agent` (subagent) tool_use calls with no matching tool_result (bug-21 pattern 2)
- **1** orphaned `Bash` tool_use at 07:41:40 (separate trace needed)

Pattern 2 is **2× more frequent than pattern 1** in this single session and fits the user-observed "happens often during research tasks" trigger far better.

## Fix shape for pattern 2

`attach.js` `_scheduleSessionKill` — on timer fire, BEFORE `killSession`:
1. Check the live AgentSession's `openToolCalls.size` (already tracked for the chat-pane "waiting on Tool · 47s" indicator).
2. If `> 0`, log `[keepalive] ${sessionId} deferring reap — ${'}n${'} tool(s) in flight` and re-schedule the timer for another 5-min slice. **Do not kill.**
3. Hard cap: if total defer time exceeds 30 min (configurable via `SESSION_MAX_DEFER_MS`), kill anyway with reason `max-defer-exceeded` — protects against a genuinely-hung tool indefinitely pinning a session.
4. Sites that touch `openToolCalls` (`agent-session.js` tool_use / tool_result branches) need no change — the Set is already mutated correctly.

Regression test for pattern 2 lands alongside the fix.

Bug stays open until pattern 2's fix ships in a follow-up commit.

comments.text:
✅ **Fully fixed** across two commits — both root causes for "research tasks deadlock" are now addressed.

## Commit 1: `0eb1289` — parallel-canUseTool deadlock (pattern 1)

When two `canUseTool` callbacks fire in quick succession (parallel WebFetch from a research subagent), the second's menu overwrote `session.pendingMenu`, the menu-broadcast supersede sweep marked the first as superseded, and the bare-digit chat shortcut could no longer reach the first. The first canUseTool resolver promise was orphaned → SDK iteration deadlocked.

Fix:
- `agent-session.js`: `pendingMenu` single-slot → `pendingMenus` `Map<hash, menu>`. Back-compat `pendingMenu` getter + new `oldestPendingMenu` getter for FIFO bare-digit.
- `agent-session.js`: `_reevaluatePendingAfterAllowAlways()` — when "Allow always" saves a rule that matches another pending menu, that menu auto-resolves with `{auto:true, reason:…}` so the user doesn't have to re-click the same decision.
- `menu.js`: removed the `_supersedeStaleMenus` call from `broadcastMenuToChat` (its serial-mode assumption breaks under parallel tool calls).
- `attach.js`: bare-digit shortcut uses `oldestPendingMenu` (FIFO head-of-queue) instead of newest.

Test: `test/bug-21-parallel-permission-menus.test.js` (16 cases).

## Commit 2: `c0596af` — reaper kills subagent mid-flight (pattern 2)

When the user disconnects (closed tab, network blip) while a long-running subagent is in its quiet model-thinking phase, the 5-min keepalive reaper killed the `AgentSession` unconditionally — the parent stream looked "idle" because subagent internal API work doesn't emit through `_emit`. The `Agent` tool_use was orphaned, no tool_result ever delivered to the parent SDK iteration.

Fix in `attach.js` `_onKillTimerFire` (extracted from `_scheduleSessionKill`):
- On timer fire, before `killSession`, read the live `AgentSession.openToolCalls.size` (already tracked for the chat-pane "waiting on Tool · 47s" indicator).
- If `> 0`: log `[keepalive] sid deferring reap — N tool(s) in flight` and re-arm another 5-min grace slice. Accumulate `totalDeferMs` on a new `_sessionDeferState` Map.
- `SESSION_MAX_DEFER_MS = 30 min` hard cap: if accumulated defer time exceeds the cap, reap anyway with reason `max-defer-exceeded` (protects against a genuinely-hung tool indefinitely pinning a session).
- `_cancelSessionKill` clears `_sessionDeferState` so fresh disconnect cycles start with a fresh 30-min window.

Test: `test/bug-21-keepalive-defers-while-tool-in-flight.test.js` (10 cases).

## Investigation summary

Evidence from this session's `_myco_/events.jsonl` (3438 events, 65 respawns):

| Pattern | Symptom in log | Frequency in this session |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1 orphaned `permission_request` (no matching `permission_resolved`) | 1× — at 05:57:17 |
| 2 | 2 orphaned `Agent` (subagent) `tool_use` with no matching `tool_result` | 2× — at 13:19:34 and 05:57:02 |
| (separate) | 1 orphaned `Bash` `tool_use` at 07:41:40 | 1× — needs separate trace, not bug-21 |

Pattern 2 is **2× more frequent** than pattern 1 here. The user-observed correlation ("happens often during long-running research tasks") is consistent with pattern 2 being the dominant trigger — subagents are the entity that issues both parallel tool calls AND have multi-minute internal phases that look idle to the parent.

## Test coverage

- `test/bug-21-parallel-permission-menus.test.js` — 16 cases (pattern 1)
- `test/bug-21-keepalive-defers-while-tool-in-flight.test.js` — 10 cases (pattern 2)
- `test/state-update.test.js` — UPDATED to reverse the pre-bug-21 contract (broadcastMenuToChat must NOT call `_supersedeStaleMenus`)
- Total regression sweep across bug-21 + 11 adjacent suites: **172 / 172 green**.

## Followups (NOT addressed by this bug — file separately if observed post-deploy)

1. **MCP stale-snapshot meta-bug** — flagged in the bug-21 text itself. Different code path (`myco-mcp.js` snapshot vs. `canUseTool` promise resolution). Worth a separate `bug-N`.
2. **Orphaned Bash tool_use at 07:41:40** — single instance, no permission_request, no result. Could be related to either pattern or a third issue. Trace separately if it recurs.
3. **`./test.sh` busybox-grep environmental issue** — predates bug-21, blocks any agent run on this host. Worth an `fr-N` to either install GNU grep on the build image or port the static checks to busybox-portable `node -e` regex.
4. **`Agent` tool result surfacing** — even when the subagent completes successfully, its result lands in the transcript jsonl but doesn't get surfaced as a chat-pane card (parent SDK only sees `Agent` as a tool_use with a tool_result blob). Worth an `fr-N` for a "subagent reply card" UI affordance — earlier flagged in td-29 / fr-7 territory.

✅ **Fixed** in commit `e482785` — rate-limit + transient-error retry shipped.

## What landed

`agent-session.js _ensureIteration` is now a `for (attempt 1..3)` retry loop. Each attempt creates a fresh `_msgQueue` + `_abortController`, drains `_pendingPrePush`, and re-spawns `query()` with `resume=sdkSessionId` so the SDK conversation continues from where it left off.

**Recoverable errors trigger retry:**
- `err.code` ∈ {`ECONNRESET`, `ETIMEDOUT`, `EAI_AGAIN`, `ENOTFOUND`, `EPIPE`} (also `err.cause.code` per Node fetch's wrap pattern).
- `err.message` matches `/rate.?limit|too many requests|\b50[234]\b|fetch failed|network|timeout/i`.

**NOT recoverable (escapes loop immediately):**
- AbortError (user-initiated Stop) → `iteration_aborted` event, break. **Never auto-retries past the user's intent.**
- 4xx auth / validation / non-transient errors → `fatal` on first attempt.

**Backoff schedule:** `[1000, 4000, 16000]` ms (1s → 4s → 16s), indexed by failed-attempt number, capped at the last slot.

**New events for observability:**
- `retry_attempt {attempt, reason, retryAfterMs, error}` emitted between attempts.
- `fatal {reason:'retry_exhausted', attempts:3}` distinguishes "tried 3 times" from a one-shot fatal.

**New helpers:** `_isRecoverable(err)` (pure classifier), `_emitRetryAndWait(attempt, backoffMs, err)` (emits event + sleeps).

## Test coverage

`test/fr-43-rate-limit-retry.test.js` — **21 cases**:

- 13 in-test behavior tests against an inlined `simulateRetryLoop` that mirrors the prod retry shape: recoverable codes, wrapped `err.cause.code`, message-pattern recovery, **AbortError exits immediately** (critical — must never auto-retry past user Stop), non-recoverable errors don't retry, 1 transient → retry → succeed, 3 transients → exhausted, mixed transient+rate_limit sequence resolves correctly.
- 8 static-grep guards anchoring the fix onto prod source: `_isRecoverable` exists + checks all 5 transient codes + matches rate-limit / `50[234]` patterns; `_ensureIteration` has a per-attempt for-loop; `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3` declared; backoff schedule `[1000, 4000, 16000]` declared; emits `retry_attempt` and `fatal`-with-`retry_exhausted`; AbortError escape preserved.

Adjacent regression sweep: **169/169 green** across fr-43 + 10 adjacent suites (stop-button-interrupt, bug-21 patterns 1+2, bug-17, state-update, chat-persistence, admin-delegation, strict-mode, persist-assistant-chat, agent-replay-dedup).

## Followups not in this commit

- The `m.retry_after` field on `rate_limit_event` is not yet read — current backoff is fixed schedule. Adding it is a one-liner inside `_emitRetryAndWait` once we verify the field shape against the SDK's `rate_limit_event` type. Worth filing as a small follow-up if you want sub-second precision on rate-limit recovery.
- The chat-pane UI doesn't yet render `retry_attempt` as a visible "retrying..." badge. The event fires on the WS frame stream; surfacing it would be a client-side change in `app.js` (likely 1-2 lines in the agent-event card renderer).

## Next: fr-44 (resume-failure fallback) ready to go on request — same surgical scope (~½ day).

items.text:
**Problem:** `agent-session.js _ensureIteration` for-await loop catches AbortError → emits `iteration_aborted`; every other error → emits `fatal` and the AgentSession iteration dies permanently. `rate_limit_event` events from the SDK are just passed through as `rate_limit` with no retry behavior. Net: a single transient API hiccup (rate-limit, network blip, model-side timeout) kills the iteration and the user has to retry manually.

**Expected:** Wrap the for-await loop with retry-on-recoverable. Read `m.retry_after` from rate_limit events, sleep, re-spawn `query()` with `resume=sdkSessionId` to continue from where we left off. Cap at 3 retries with exponential backoff (1s → 4s → 16s). Same retry path catches network blips mid-stream. Emit `retry_attempt {attempt, reason, retryAfterMs}` events for observability.

**Done when:**
- `_ensureIteration` has a retry loop that handles `rate_limit_event` mid-stream (sleep retry_after, respawn with resume=, increment attempt counter).
- Same retry loop catches transient network errors (`ECONNRESET`, `ETIMEDOUT`, fetch failures) and retries with backoff.
- AbortError / abortController abort still escapes the retry loop (user-initiated stop must not auto-retry).
- Cap at 3 attempts; on exhaustion emit `fatal` with `{reason:'retry_exhausted', attempts:3, lastErr}`.
- Regression test `test/fr-43-rate-limit-retry.test.js` verifies: 1 transient → retry → succeed; 3 transients → fatal; AbortError exits immediately.

**Pri/Eff:** P1 / S (~½ day).

**Source:** SDK best-practices research §1 (highest user-visible reliability win); td-29 multica audit pattern "Rate-limit + transient-error retry"; precedent in multica daemon (idle-watchdog + retry-on-resume-failure).

✅ **Fixed** in commit `193e73d` (alongside fr-45 — closely-linked SDK robustness work).

## What landed

`agent-session.js` got a new `_isResumeFailure(err)` helper and a resume-fallback branch in `_ensureIteration`. When the SDK rejects `resume=sdkSessionId` because the upstream conversation is gone (container restart wiped `$HOME/.claude/projects/`, sessionId corrupted on disk, transcript file deleted under us):

1. Emit `resume_failed {prevSdkSessionId, error}` event for observability.
2. Clear `this.sdkSessionId`.
3. Clear `rec.sdkSessionId` via `sessionsMod.saveStore()` so a server restart doesn't re-attempt the same stale resume.
4. `attempt--` so this doesn't count against `MAX_ATTEMPTS`, then `continue` — the next iteration's `sdkOpts.resume = …` line skips (`this.sdkSessionId` is null), `query()` spawns a fresh SDK conversation.

The `!!sdkOpts.resume` clause is itself the one-shot guard: after the first fire, sdkSessionId is null so `resume=` isn't set on subsequent attempts, and this branch self-disables. No infinite loop on repeated "session not found" errors.

Pattern set (conservative): `/session not found | invalid session.?id | could not load conversation | conversation not found | no such session | session expired/i`. New phrases observed in production should be added here.

Multica precedent: `daemon/wakeup.go resolveSessionID()`.

## Test coverage

`test/fr-44-resume-failure-fallback.test.js` — **16 cases**:

- 11 in-test behavior tests: `_isResumeFailure` matches each documented phrase, rejects unrelated errors (network / AbortError / generic), null/undefined defensive; fallback fires on stale ID + clears it + retries with fresh session; no-fallback when there's no sdkSessionId (otherwise we'd loop forever on a generic "not found" error); one-shot guard prevents infinite loop on repeated resume failures; AbortError exits before fallback can fire.
- 5 static-grep guards anchoring the fix onto prod source.

## Verification

- Standalone: 16/16 green.
- Full dynamic-test sweep: 37/37 suites pass (~412 cases), no regression in adjacent suites.
- Full `./test.sh` blocked by the unrelated busybox-grep environmental issue (predates this work; reported separately in bug-21 followups).

## What's NOT in this commit (followups)

- Reading a structured "resume failed" error class if the SDK introduces one — current detection is message-pattern only. Lower bar to add a class check if/when the SDK exposes one.
- Surfacing `resume_failed` events as a chat-pane note ("the previous conversation context was lost; starting fresh"). One-liner addition in `app.js` agent-event renderer.

items.text:
**Problem:** `agent-session.js _ensureIteration` passes `sdkOpts.resume = this.sdkSessionId` unconditionally. If the SDK rejects the resume (session expired upstream, session_id corrupted, container restarted with a different SDK install, conversation transcript wiped from `$HOME/.claude/projects/`), the initial `query()` call throws → `fatal` event → AgentSession is dead until manually respawned. This is the bug-1 / post-redeploy-resume-hang pattern.

**Expected:** Wrap the initial `stream = query(opts)` in a try/catch that distinguishes "resume failed" errors (string match on `error.message` containing "session not found" / "invalid session_id" / "could not load conversation", or a typed error class if the SDK exposes one). On match: `rec.sdkSessionId = null; saveStore(); emit 'resume_failed' {prevSdkSessionId}`; retry once with fresh sdkSessionId (no resume= field). Fresh session loses prior conversation context but at least the iteration starts and the user can continue.

**Done when:**
- `_ensureIteration` has a resume-failure catch around the FIRST `query()` call that doesn't fire on the retry attempt (so we don't infinite-loop on a fundamentally-broken SDK).
- On resume failure: clear `rec.sdkSessionId` via sessionsMod.saveStore(), emit `resume_failed` event, retry once with no resume.
- Multica precedent: `daemon/wakeup.go resolveSessionID()` does exactly this.
- Regression test `test/fr-44-resume-failure-fallback.test.js` verifies: corrupted sdkSessionId → first query() throws → catch → clear → retry succeeds; legitimate resume → no retry; resume-failure on retry → fatal (no infinite loop).

**Pri/Eff:** P1 / S (~½ day).

**Source:** SDK best-practices research §2; td-29 multica audit pattern "Session lifecycle/resume"; original bug-1 closure was "PTY-era obsolete" but the underlying class of failure can recur in SDK mode.

✅ **Fixed** in commit `193e73d` (alongside fr-44).

## What landed

`agent-session.js` got two new things:

1. `SDK_OPTIONS_ALLOWLIST` — module-level `Set` of 61 documented SDK Options field names extracted from `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts` at the `sdk@0.3.142` version pinned in `server/package.json`.
2. `_validateSdkOpts(opts)` method that walks `Object.keys(opts)`, flags any key not in the allowlist via `console.error('[sdkOpts] sid UNKNOWN options (likely typos — SDK will silently drop these): X, Y, Z')`. Loud enough to catch in `/loop` log scans + the `_myco_/logs/` capture.

Wired into `_ensureIteration` immediately before the `query()` call — BEFORE the SDK's silent-drop fires.

## Design choices

- **Logs, does NOT throw.** We'd rather have the SDK call proceed (with some feature subtly broken) than crash the iteration outright. The regression test surfaces typos pre-deploy; the runtime log is the safety net.
- **Hardcoded allowlist** (vs runtime-extracted from sdk.d.ts) — simpler, no parser dependency. Trade-off: must be manually synced across SDK minor-version bumps. The fr-45 regression test pins the 9 myco-used keys' presence, so a sync drift surfaces as a test failure.

## Test coverage

`test/fr-45-sdkopts-lint.test.js` — **13 cases**:

- 7 in-test behavior tests: allowlist contains all 9 myco-used keys; `abortSignal` (the bug-14 round 2 typo) is flagged; arbitrary typo like `canUseTools` (plural) flagged; empty/null/undefined safe; mixed known + unknown reports ALL unknowns.
- 6 static-grep guards: `SDK_OPTIONS_ALLOWLIST` declared at module level, contains all 9 myco-used keys, **does NOT contain `abortSignal`** (negative guard against a careless refactor adding it back), `_validateSdkOpts` exists + called from `_ensureIteration` BEFORE `query()`, surfaces via `console.error/warn`.

## Why this matters

The bug-14 round 2 lesson: SDK options are plain JS object keys and a typo is silently dropped by the SDK with no warning. `abortSignal` instead of `abortController` sat undetected for ~3 weeks until a user reported Stop didn't work. The same risk exists for every other sdkOpts key — 9 currently in use × ~61 valid SDK fields × typo probability = a real future-bug surface area. The validator closes it.

## Verification

- Standalone: 13/13 green.
- Full dynamic-test sweep: 37/37 suites pass (~412 cases), no regression.

## What's NOT in this commit (followups)

- Auto-syncing the allowlist against the live `sdk.d.ts` on package update — would need a small build-time extractor. Worth a follow-up FR if SDK minor bumps start happening frequently enough that manual maintenance becomes a chore.
- Dev-mode throw (`if NODE_ENV !== 'production'`) so tests in CI crash on typos instead of just logging. Worth considering once we have a CI pipeline that actually runs `./test.sh` (currently blocked by the busybox-grep issue).

items.text:
**Problem:** SDK options are passed as JS object keys (`sdkOpts.canUseTool`, `sdkOpts.hooks`, etc.). A typo in any field name is silently ignored by the SDK — no warning, no runtime error, just a feature that doesn't work. This is exactly how bug-14 round 2 sat undetected for ~3 weeks: we passed `abortSignal: controller.signal` when the documented field is `abortController: controller`. The SDK silently dropped the unknown field and Stop became a no-op until the user reported it. Same risk exists for every other field: `permissionMode`, `settings`, `settingSources`, `mcpServers`, `additionalDirectories`, `agents`, `includePartialMessages`, etc. ~15 fields total, any silent typo is a future bug-14.

**Expected:** Add a validator that explicitly checks each `sdkOpts` key against the SDK's documented `Options` type. Either:
(a) Hardcoded allowlist of known SDK option names (~15 strings), checked at the top of `_ensureIteration`. Unknown key → `console.error('[sdkOpts] unknown option: X — likely typo')` + (in dev mode) throw.
(b) OR a startup-time assertion that loads `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts` once, extracts the Options keys at boot, validates `sdkOpts` against that set on every `query()` call. Heavier but auto-tracks SDK version bumps.

**Done when:**
- One of the two validator shapes lands in `agent-session.js` _before_ the `query()` call.
- Validator allowlist matches the actual `Options` type from the SDK version pinned in `package.json`.
- Regression test `test/fr-45-sdkopts-lint.test.js` verifies: known-good options pass; `abortSignal` (the bug-14 typo) fails loudly; missing required options pass (the validator only flags UNKNOWN keys, not missing ones); validator survives SDK minor-version bumps (option set is reloadable).
- Bonus: bug-14 regression test stays green (the `abortController:` static guard already exists, this is belt-and-suspenders).

**Pri/Eff:** P1 / M (~1 day).

**Source:** SDK best-practices research §3 — the bug-14 round 2 lesson. Without this, the next silent-typo bug will look exactly like the last one: passing tests, no errors, broken behavior in production until a user notices.

✅ **Shipped** in commit `43957ce` — inline edit for item body text + comment edit/delete, owner+admin only.

## Server (`server/src/artifacts.js`)

| Route | Auth | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `PATCH /sessions/:id/artifact/item?type=plan&itemId=…` | `isOwnerOrAdmin` | Updates `item.text`. Stamps `meta.editedBy` + `meta.editedAt`. Preserves `meta.originalText` on **first edit only** (later edits don't overwrite the snapshot — fat-finger rewrites stay recoverable). |
| `PATCH /sessions/:id/artifact/comment?type=plan&itemId=…&commentId=…` | `isOwnerOrAdmin` | Updates `comment.text`. Stamps `comment.meta.editedBy` + `editedAt`. |
| `DELETE /sessions/:id/artifact/comment` (extended) | author **OR** `isOwnerOrAdmin` | Author-self-delete preserved. NEW: owner+admin can delete any comment. |

All three persist + broadcast via existing `persistArtifact` + `broadcastArtifact` helpers — connected clients see edits in-flight without a refresh.

## Client (`web/public/app.js` + `styles.css`)

- **Item card actions row** — pencil button (✎) next to ▶ Run and × Delete. Visible only when `!state.readOnly` (owner+admin). Click → swap the rendered markdown body for an inline `<textarea>` + Save/Cancel. Save fires PATCH; Cancel reloads to restore the markdown preview.
- **Comment row** — two new buttons per comment (pencil + trash). Same `!state.readOnly` gate. Pencil → inline textarea (mirrors item editor). Trash → `confirm()` → DELETE.

Hover title shows editor + ISO timestamp; for items with preserved `originalText`, mentions that the original is recoverable.

## Test coverage (`test/fr-46-edit-plan-items.test.js`, 24 cases)

- **16 in-test behavior tests** against inlined `patchItem`/`patchComment`/`deleteComment` helpers mirroring prod handlers: owner edits, admin edits, viewer 403s, `meta.editedBy` stamping, `originalText` preserved on FIRST edit only (not overwritten by later edits), empty-text rejection, unknown-id 404, author-self-delete preserved, NEW owner-delete-any, NEW admin-delete-any, random-viewer-403.
- **8 static-grep guards** anchoring the fix onto prod source: both PATCH routes declared; both gate on `isOwnerOrAdmin`; DELETE comment now uses `isOwnerOrAdmin`; item-edit stamps `editedBy` + `originalText`; `app.js` fires PATCH from new save handlers; `app.js` gates affordances on `!state.readOnly`.

✅ **Shipped** in commit `c0100c3` — explicit Close/Reopen button replaces the dual-purpose checkbox.

## What changed

Pre-fix the `.artifact-item-checkbox` did TWO semantically distinct things based on direction:
- **Check** a plan item → `action='run'` → POST `/artifact/run` → dispatched to claude
- **Uncheck** → `action='mark'` → POST `/artifact/mark` → set `done=false`

After fr-48 unified dispatch through the queue (▶ Run button now POSTs `/queue/add`), the check-to-run path was redundant — and the dual-purpose affordance was confusing: same widget, two meanings.

## Replacement

| State | Button | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `!it.done` | **Close** | POST `/artifact/mark?done=1` — pure lifecycle toggle, no claude dispatch |
| `it.done` | **Reopen** | POST `/artifact/mark?done=0` — clears done state |

The button carries `data-done` reflecting CURRENT state; click flips to the opposite. State-update fans out via WS → `renderArtifact` re-renders with the new label.

## Visual treatment
- Same chip style as ✎ Edit / × Delete buttons (transparent, 11px, accent on hover)
- `.is-done .artifact-item-close` gets a warm orange tint to distinguish Reopen at a glance

## Removed
- `onArtifactItemToggle` function (no callers since checkbox is gone — BP §1)
- `.artifact-item-checkbox` CSS rule

## Test coverage (`test/fr-47-close-open-affordance.test.js`, 6 cases)

1. `artifact-item-checkbox` no longer in source
2. `artifact-item-close` button rendered
3. Literal strings "Close" + "Reopen" both present
4. `onArtifactItemClose` POSTs to `/artifact/mark`
5. **Negative guard:** `onArtifactItemClose` does NOT POST to `/artifact/run` (prevents re-introducing the conflated path)
6. `onArtifactItemToggle` removed (no dead code)

Verified: **175/175** across fr-47 + 14 adjacent suites (fr-46, all 5 fr-48 variants, plan-item-run-button, plan-run-comment, state-update, admin, strict-mode, chat-routing, stop-button, bug-21).

## Net architecture after fr-47 + fr-48

| Action | Affordance | Route |
|---|---|---|
| **Run item now** (dispatch to claude) | ▶ Implement / ▶ Fix / ▶ Do button | POST /queue/add (enqueue + kick if idle) |
| **Close item** (mark done, no dispatch) | Close button | POST /artifact/mark?done=1 |
| **Reopen item** (clear done) | Reopen button | POST /artifact/mark?done=0 |
| **Edit body** | ✎ pencil → inline textarea | PATCH /artifact/item |
| **Delete item** | × | DELETE /artifact/item |

Single-purpose affordances throughout.

**Problem:** No way to queue multiple fr/td/bug items for myco to work through sequentially; users must hand-feed them one at a time.
**Expected:** Users can add fr/td/bug items to a queue and myco picks them up one by one; the chat pane shows the queue with per-task status.
**Actual:** Only single-item execution is supported and the chat pane has no queue/status view.

comments.text:
✅ **Shipped** in commit `af45609` — per-session plan-item run-queue with sequential auto-dispatch, persistent chat-pane chip strip, and 5 slash commands.

**Note on the earlier `done:true` state:** fr-48 was previously marked done with a `ranAt` timestamp but zero comments and no implementation — likely the recurring MCP stale-snapshot bug or an accidental Done click. This is the real closure.

## Why this matters

Walking away from myco wasn't possible before: every fr/td/bug item needed file → ▶ Run → wait → file next → ▶ Run → wait. fr-48 enables a "queue 5 items and come back when they're done" workflow.

## New module: `server/src/runQueue.js`

Pure functional state mutation, no I/O. 9 exports. Data shape on the rec:

```js
rec.runQueue = [{ itemId, type, status, addedAt, addedBy, startedAt?, finishedAt? }]
rec.runQueuePaused = boolean
```

Status transitions: `pending → running → success | failed | cancelled`. Failure auto-pauses the queue; `peekNextPending` returns null until `resumeQueue`. Re-adding an itemId that previously finished is ALLOWED (re-runs are common). Re-adding a pending/running itemId throws.

## Server

**4 HTTP routes in `artifacts.js`**, all gated on `isOwnerOrAdmin`:

| Route | What |
|---|---|
| `POST /sessions/:id/queue/add {itemId,type}` | Append + kick if idle |
| `DELETE /sessions/:id/queue/:itemId` | Remove pending entry |
| `POST /sessions/:id/queue/clear` | Drop all pending |
| `POST /sessions/:id/queue/resume` | Unpause + dispatch next |

All persist + broadcast `state-update {kind:'runQueue'}`.

**5 slash commands in `slashcmds.js`:**

```
/queue fr-43 [fr-44 ...]   — add items
/qstatus                   — print queue
/qcancel fr-43             — remove pending
/qclear                    — drop all pending
/qresume                   — unpause + dispatch next
```

All owner+admin gated via `_requireQueueAuth`. `/queue` and `/qresume` ALSO kick dispatch if the queue head would be the next pending entry (same logic as the HTTP `/queue/add` route).

**`attach.js` turn_result auto-advance hook:** `_registerExternalSession`'s existing turn_result listener now also calls `_advanceRunQueue`. If the just-finished item is the queue's running head, mark its outcome and dispatch the next pending entry via the same `handleChatMessage` + `buildArtifactRunText` path the manual ▶ Run button uses. On failure: pause + emit a chat note explaining `/qresume` to continue. Manual ▶ Run clicks (not from queue) silently no-op the hook.

## Client (`app.js` + `styles.css`)

- **Per-item ⊤ Queue button** on each plan item card next to ▶ Run. Gated on `!state.readOnly && !it.done`. POST /queue/add on click.
- **Persistent chip strip** pinned to the top of the chat pane (`#runqueue-strip`). One chip per queue entry with glyph + itemId + status class (running gets a green tint, failed red, success faded, cancelled strike-through). Click chip → `location.hash` jumps to the plan item card. × cancel button on pending entries. Resume/Clear buttons appear when paused/has-pending. Strip hides when queue is empty.
- **`state-update {kind:'runQueue'}` handler** caches the latest state into `state.runQueue` + re-renders the strip.

## Test coverage (`test/fr-48-run-queue.test.js`, 22 cases)

- 14 in-test behavior tests against the real `runQueue` module (loaded via `require` — tests the actual production logic, not a re-implementation): add/remove/clear, dedupe rejection, re-add after terminal, refuse-to-remove-running, FIFO peek, paused = null peek, status transitions, failure auto-pauses + resume restores.
- 8 static-grep guards anchoring all 4 queue routes + isOwnerOrAdmin gating, 5 slash commands + handler functions, attach.js turn_result hook references `runQueue`, app.js renders `artifact-item-queue` button + calls POST /queue/add + handles `state-update {kind:'runQueue'}`.

## Verification

Regression sweep: **292/292 tests pass** across fr-48 + 20 adjacent suites (all fr-43/44/45/46, bug-21 patterns 1+2, bug-17, state-update, admin/strict/chat/persist/replay, all plan-* suites).

## Followups documented for later (not in this commit)

1. **Manual /qpause command** — currently the only way to pause is via failure-auto-pause. A user-initiated `/qpause` (or pause button on the chip strip) would let you halt a queue mid-flight without canceling everything.
2. **dependsOn auto-queue** — when adding an item whose `dependsOn` chain has unmet prereqs, optionally auto-queue the prereqs first so the head item is dispatched only after they finish. Currently you have to hand-queue each prereq.
3. **Queue position drag-reorder in UI** — pending entries are FIFO based on add-order. A drag-reorder affordance in the chip strip would let users re-prioritize without /qcancel + re-add.
4. **Per-user queues vs per-session queue** — fr-48 ships per-session. If multi-user sessions get heavy and people queue against each other, per-user-scoped queues might matter. Worth observing first.

🔧 **Follow-up:** dispatch unified through the queue in commit `606f14c`.

Per user feedback: "Clicking fix/do/implement should add to the queue; claude should always get the task from the queue."

Pre-unification fr-48 had TWO dispatch paths — the ▶ Run button bypassed the queue (composed `[run:plan#<id>]` marker into chat input and auto-submitted), while only the ⊤ Queue button + slash commands used the queue. Meant clicking Run while items were pending would jump the queue.

## What changed

- New shared helper `_enqueueAndKickIfIdle(ctx, type, itemId, user, opts)` in `artifacts.js` — the single dispatch path. Adds to queue + kicks the head if idle.
- **▶ Run button** (`onArtifactItemRun` in app.js) now POSTs `/queue/add` (was: chat-input marker auto-submit).
- **POST /artifact/run server route** now enqueues via the helper (was: direct `handleChatMessage`). Response shape preserved + adds `{queued:true, kicked:bool}` fields. Auth tightened to require `isOwnerOrAdmin` (was: viewer-tier preamble).
- **`autoFireIfQuorum`** (vote auto-execute at threshold) now enqueues via the helper, passing the quorum-flavored chat text as `opts.text` so the "voted by @X, @Y" social context still surfaces.

Test `test/fr-48-unified-dispatch.test.js` (4 cases) anchors the contract. Regression sweep: 296/296 across fr-48 + fr-48-unified + 20 adjacent suites.

The ⊤ Queue button on each item card is now functionally redundant with ▶ Run — both POST `/queue/add`. Left in place for explicit-intent discoverability; flag if you want it pruned.

items.text:
**Problem:** No surfaced queue of "what to implement next" — priorities have to be re-derived manually each session.
**Expected:** A list of suggested next items ranked by priority and importance, refreshed periodically by myco every 2 hours.
**Actual:** No such list exists; suggestions are ad-hoc.

comments.text: the list should be persisted in plan.json and displayed via /whatsnext command in the chat pane, suggest a better shortcut for "whatsnext"

**Shipped.** `/whatsnext` (alias **`/next`**) returns a ranked list of open plan items, cached in `plan.whatsNext` with a 2-hour refresh-on-read TTL.

### Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  call["/whatsnext\nor /next"] --> handle["handleWhatsNext"]
  handle --> getCached["whatsnext.getCachedOrGenerate"]
  getCached --> fresh{"< 2h old?"}
  fresh -->|yes| reply["chat reply"]
  fresh -->|no| heur["computeHeuristicShortlist\n(top 20 by score)"]
  heur --> llm["rerankWithLLM\nvia btw.runClaudeP"]
  llm -->|ok| persist["plan.whatsNext = {items, generatedAt, llmReranked:true}"]
  llm -->|timeout/fail| persistH["plan.whatsNext = {items, generatedAt, llmReranked:false}"]
  persist --> reply
  persistH --> reply
```

### Heuristic scoring (`server/src/whatsnext.js`)

| Factor | Weight | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Each unique voter | **+3.0** | Strongest signal of intent |
| Each comment | +1.0 (capped at 5) | Diminishing returns so chatty items don't dominate |
| Layer bias | Bug 2.0 · Feature 1.0 · Todo 0.5 | Bug > Feature > Todo by default |
| Fresh (<7d) | **+2.0** | Recency boost |
| Stale (>90d) | **−0.5** | Decay penalty |
| Last run failed/aborted | **−1.5** | Stuck items shouldn't lead |
| Done / in-queue | excluded | No re-suggesting completed or already-queued work |

Each entry surfaces its **reasons** array (e.g. `"2 voters (+6.0), Bug bias (+2.0), fresh <7d (+2.0)"`) so the user can see WHY an item ranked where it did.

### LLM rerank (best-effort)

Top 20 heuristic candidates → `btw.runClaudeP` (in-process SDK side-channel, 60s timeout, no tools, no chat pollution) with a rubric: user-facing bugs first, then small unblock-other-work fixes, then engagement-heavy features, then cleanup. The parser:
- accepts `1. id` / `1) id` / `- id` / bare `id` forms
- **rejects hallucinated ids** (any id not in the input list is discarded)
- dedups + appends omitted ids at the tail in heuristic order so nothing disappears

Any LLM failure (timeout, no key, garbage reply) falls back to the heuristic order silently — `/whatsnext` always returns something useful.

### Slash command (`/whatsnext` + `/next`)

- Read-only — works for owners, admins, AND read-only guests (added to both `attach.js GUEST_ALLOWED_CMDS` and `app.js _GUEST_ALLOWED_CMDS` per the bug-19 send-button-gate mirror contract)
- `/next force` (or `--refresh`) regenerates ignoring cache
- Reply format: numbered top-10 entries with score · layer · reasons · snippet, header includes "generated Nm ago" + LLM-vs-heuristic tag, footer reminds about the cache TTL

### Discoverability suggestion

You asked me to suggest a better shortcut — **`/next`** (4 chars, intuitive, conflict-free with the queue-namespaced /q* commands). Primary alias; `/whatsnext` is the more discoverable secondary. Both fire `handleWhatsNext`.

### Test
